<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> whether we learned our faith at the knee of our mama's mama,<|5.00|>
<|5.90|> or we are awakening to God for the very first time here and now,<|12.20|>
<|12.90|> we need each other.<|14.56|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Paul needed the support of community and the hospitality of strangers as he journeyed to<|6.56|>
<|6.56|> spread the gospel of Christ.<|9.58|>
<|9.58|> Paul needed the meals and the care brought to him by his followers when he was imprisoned.<|16.46|>
<|16.46|> Paul needed the companionship of Timothy as he traveled, and he needed, as we're learning<|24.10|>
<|24.10|> in this letter, to pass on his wisdom to his student.<|28.60|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We, too, need companions on the way.<|5.18|>
<|5.52|> We need our family of origin and or our family of choice.<|11.46|>
<|12.28|> We need our community.<|13.90|>
<|14.42|> And for many of us, it is this place, St. John's,<|18.00|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> where our faith is tested and practiced and sustained.<|5.74|>
<|7.50|> Paul is grateful to God when he remembers Timothy constantly in his prayers, night and day.<|16.46|>
<|18.32|> I wonder, for whom are you grateful for?<|24.10|>
